Class: Geocoder

(abstract) bemap.Geocoder(context, options)

Base class for geocoder.

Constructor

(abstract) new Geocoder(context, options)

Parameters:
Name Type Description
context bemap.Context BeMap-JS-API Context. Mandatory.
options object see below the available values.
Properties
Name Type Description
language String Define the language that will be used to perform the address lookup.
maxResult Int The maximum number of items used to perform the research and returned items by the server. Default is 1.
boundingBox bemap.BoundingBox the bounding box that will limit the research to a specific area.
Source:
  • bemap-services/bemap-geocoder.js, line 17

cleanMarkers() → {bemap.markerMapObject}

Reset the geocoding marker object. Clear the previous result.

this
Type
bemap.markerMapObject

createTable(response, options, container, listener)

Create table in selected div with data from parsing. Event click send by listener
Parameters:
Name Type Description
response Object data for create table.
options object see below the available values.
container DIV element to create table inside.
listener function the function to call to get click data
Source:
  • bemap-services/bemap-geocoder.js, line 316

geocode(request, optionsopt) → {Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse>|bemap.Geocoder}

Geocode a structured or free-text request.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
request bemap.GeocodingRequest | Object When a `GeocodingRequest` is passed the call returns a Promise. Any other shape falls through to the legacy callback-style implementation.
options Object <optional>
Properties
Name Type Attributes Description
signal AbortSignal <optional>
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/search/bemap-geocoder-v2.js, line 47
Returns:
Type
Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse> | bemap.Geocoder

geocode(options) → {bemap.Geocoder}

Send a geocoding request to the bemap's server.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
options object See below the available values.
Properties
Name Type Description
searchInfo bemap.GeoSearchInfo the information to to search.
geoserver object Geoserver name will be used for this computation.
success function the function to call in case of successed request.
failed function the function to call in case of failed request.
Source:
  • bemap-services/bemap-geocoder.js, line 186
Returns:
this.
Type
bemap.Geocoder

reverseGeocode(coordinate, radiusopt, optionsopt) → {Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse>}

Convenience alias for the typed reverse-geocoding path.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Default Description
coordinate bemap.Coordinate | bemap.CoordinateSat
radius Number <optional>
 50
options Object <optional>
 Extra fields forwarded to ReverseGeocodingRequest.
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/search/bemap-geocoder-v2.js, line 120
Returns:
Type
Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse>

revGeocode(request, optionsopt) → {Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse>|bemap.Geocoder}

Reverse-geocode through the same channel `bemap.ReverseGeocoder` uses (`POST service/geocoding/1.0/reverse`). Accepts either a `ReverseGeocodingRequest` (typed v1.5 path) or the legacy `{searchInfo: RevGeoSearchInfo, success, failed}` shape.
Parameters:
Name Type Attributes Description
request bemap.ReverseGeocodingRequest | Object
options Object <optional>
Source:
  • bemap-services-v2/search/bemap-geocoder-v2.js, line 100
Returns:
Type
Promise.<bemap.GeocodingResponse> | bemap.Geocoder

revGeocode(options) → {bemap.Geocoder}

Execute the geocoding research.
Parameters:
Name Type Description
options object See below the available values.
Properties
Name Type Description
searchInfo bemap.RevGeoSearchInfo the information to to search.
geoserver object Geoserver name will be used for this computation.
success function the function to call in case of successed request.
failed function the function to call in case of failed request.
Source:
  • bemap-services/bemap-geocoder.js, line 151
Returns:
this.
Type
bemap.Geocoder
